1:
In the eighth month, 
in the second year of Darius, the word of the 
Lord
 came to the prophet 
Zechariah, the son of 
Berechiah, son of 
Iddo, saying,
A Call to Return to the Lord
2:
“The 
Lord
 was very angry with your fathers.
A Call to Return to the Lord
3:
Therefore say to them, Thus declares the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
Return to me, says the 
Lord
 of hosts, and 
I will return to you, says the 
Lord
 of hosts.
A Call to Return to the Lord
4:
Do not be like your fathers, 
to whom the former prophets cried out, ‘Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts, 
Return from your evil ways and from your evil deeds.’ But 
they did not hear or pay attention to me, declares the 
Lord
.
A Call to Return to the Lord
; [Ps. 78:8]; See 2 Chr. 36:15; [See ver. 3 above]; ch. 7:11; 2 Chr. 36:16; See Jer. 35:15
5:
Your fathers, where are they? And 
the prophets, do they live forever?
A Call to Return to the Lord
[John 8:52]
6:
But my words and my statutes, which I commanded 
my servants the prophets, did they not 
overtake your fathers? So they repented and said, 
‘As the 
Lord
 of hosts purposed to deal with us for 
our ways and 
deeds, so has he dealt with us.’”
A Vision of a Horseman
7:
On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month, which is the month of Shebat, in the second year of Darius, the word of the 
Lord
 came to the prophet 
Zechariah, the son of 
Berechiah, son of 
Iddo, saying,
A Vision of a Horseman
; [See ver. 1 above]
8:
“I saw in the night, and behold, 
a man riding on a red horse! He was standing among the myrtle trees in the glen, and behind him were 
red, sorrel, and white horses.
A Vision of a Horseman
9:
Then I said, ‘What are these, my lord?’ 
The angel who talked with me said to me, ‘I will show you what they are.’
A Vision of a Horseman
10:
So 
the man who was standing among the myrtle trees answered, 
‘These are they whom the 
Lord
 has sent to 
patrol the earth.’
A Vision of a Horseman
[Heb. 1:14]; ; [See ver. 8 above]; ch. 6:7; [Job 1:7]
11:
And they answered 
the angel of the 
Lord
 who was standing among the myrtle trees, and said, 
‘We have patrolled the earth, and behold, all the earth 
remains at rest.’
A Vision of a Horseman
; [See ver. 9 above]; [ver. 15]; [See ver. 10 above]
12:
Then 
the angel of the 
Lord
 said, 
‘O 
Lord
 of hosts, 
how long will you 
have no mercy on Jerusalem and the cities of Judah, against which you have been angry these 
seventy years?’
A Vision of a Horseman
13:
And the 
Lord
 answered 
gracious and comforting words to 
the angel who talked with me.
A Vision of a Horseman
; [See ver. 9 above]; See Jer. 29:10, 11
14:
So 
the angel who talked with me said to me, ‘Cry out, Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
I am exceedingly jealous for Jerusalem and for Zion.
A Vision of a Horseman
; [See ver. 9 above]; ch. 8:2; Joel 2:18
16:
Therefore, thus says the 
Lord
, 
I have returned to Jerusalem with mercy; 
my house shall be built in it, declares the 
Lord
 of hosts, and 
the measuring line shall be stretched out over Jerusalem.
17:
Cry out again, Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
My cities shall again overflow with prosperity, 
and the 
Lord
 will again comfort Zion and again 
choose Jerusalem.’”
A Vision of Horns and Craftsmen
18:
And I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, 
four horns!
A Vision of Horns and Craftsmen
19:
And I said to 
the angel who talked with me, “What are these?” And he said to me, 
“These are the horns that have scattered Judah, Israel, and Jerusalem.”
A Vision of Horns and Craftsmen
20:
Then the 
Lord
 showed me four craftsmen.
A Vision of Horns and Craftsmen
21:
And I said, “What are these coming to do?” He said, 
“These are the horns that scattered Judah, so that no one raised his head. And these have come 
to terrify them, to cast down the horns of the nations 
who lifted up their horns against the land of Judah to scatter it.”
A Vision of Horns and Craftsmen

1:
And I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, 
a man with a measuring line in his hand!
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
Ezek. 40:3; Rev. 11:1; 2:1  Ch 2:5 in Hebrew
2:
Then I said, “Where are you going?” And he said to me, 
“To measure Jerusalem, to see what is its width and what is its length.”
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
3:
And behold, 
the angel who talked with me came forward, and another angel came forward to meet him
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
4:
and said to him, “Run, say to that young man, 
‘Jerusalem shall be inhabited 
as villages without walls, because of 
the multitude of people and livestock in it.
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line

a wall of fire all around, declares the 
Lord
, and I will be the glory in her midst.’”
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line

Flee from the land of the north, declares the 
Lord
. For I have 
spread you abroad as the four winds of the heavens, declares the 
Lord
.
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line

A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
8:
For thus said the 
Lord
 of hosts, after his glory sent me
 to the nations who plundered you, 
for he who touches you touches 
the apple of his eye:

“Behold, 
I will shake my hand over them, 
and they shall become plunder for those who served them. Then 
you will know that the 
Lord
 of hosts has sent me.
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line

Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Zion, for 
behold, I come 
and I will dwell in your midst, declares the 
Lord
.
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
11:
And many nations shall join themselves to the 
Lord
 in that day, and shall be my people. 
And I will dwell in your midst, and 
you shall know that the 
Lord
 of hosts has sent me to you.
A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line
[See ver. 10 above]; ; ch. 8:22; See Isa. 2:2; [See ver. 9 above]

Lord
 will inherit Judah as his portion in the holy land, and will again

A Vision of a Man with a Measuring Line

Lord
, for he has roused himself from his holy dwelling.

“Let them put a clean turban on his head.” So they put a clean turban on his head and clothed him with garments. And the angel of the

 was standing by.

Lord

 of hosts: If you will walk in my ways and

the Branch.

the stone that I have set before Joshua, on a single stone with

 I will

 of hosts, and 
I will remove the iniquity of this land in a single day.

 of hosts, every one of you will invite his neighbor to come

 that is going out.” And he said, “This is their iniquity

1:

Lord
 came to Zechariah on the fourth day of the ninth month, which is 
Chislev.

to entreat the favor of the 
Lord
,
A Call for Justice and Mercy

saying to the priests of the house of the 
Lord
 of hosts and 
the prophets, “Should I weep and 
abstain in 
the fifth month, as I have done for so many years?”
A Call for Justice and Mercy

Then the word of the 
Lord
 of hosts came to me:
A Call for Justice and Mercy

“Say to all the people of the land and the priests, ‘When you fasted and mourned in 
the fifth month and in 
the seventh, for these 
seventy years, 
was it 
for me that you fasted?

[2 Kgs. 25:25]; [Isa. 58:4, 5]; See ch. 1:12; [See ver. 3 above]
6: And when you eat and when you drink, do you not eat for yourselves and drink for yourselves?
A Call for Justice and Mercy

Were not these the words that the 
Lord
 proclaimed 
by the former prophets, when Jerusalem was inhabited and prosperous, 
with her cities around her, and the 
South and the 
lowland were inhabited?’”

And the word of the 
Lord
 came to Zechariah, saying,
A Call for Justice and Mercy

Lord
 of hosts, 
Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy to one another,
A Call for Justice and Mercy

or the poor, and 
let none of you devise evil against another in your heart.”

they refused to pay attention 
and turned a stubborn shoulder and stopped their ears that they might not hear.

Lord
 of hosts.

 called, and they would not hear,

Lord
 of hosts,

1:
And the word of the 
Lord
 of hosts came, saying,
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
2:
“Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
I am jealous for Zion with great jealousy, and I am jealous for her with great wrath.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
3:
Thus says the 
Lord
: 
I have returned to Zion and 
will dwell in the midst of Jerusalem, 
and Jerusalem shall be called the faithful city, 
and the mountain of the 
Lord
 of hosts, the holy mountain.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
4:
Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
Old men and old women shall again sit in the streets of Jerusalem, each with staff in hand because of great age.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
5: And the streets of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in its streets.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
6:
Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
If it is marvelous in the sight of the remnant of this people in those days, 
should it also be marvelous in my sight, declares the 
Lord
 of hosts?
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
7:
Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: Behold, 
I will save my people 
from the east country and from the west country,
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
8:
and I will bring them to dwell in the midst of Jerusalem. 
And they shall be my people, and I will be their God, 
in faithfulness and in righteousness.”
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
9:
Thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
“Let your hands be strong, you who in these days have been hearing these words from the mouth of 
the prophets who were present on 
the day that the foundation of the house of the 
Lord
 of hosts was laid, that the temple might be built.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
10:
For before those days 
there was no wage for man or any wage for beast, neither was there any safety from the foe for him who went out or came in, for I set every man against his neighbor.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
11:
But now I will not deal with the remnant of this people as in the former days, declares the 
Lord
 of hosts.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
12:
For there shall be a sowing of peace. The vine shall give its fruit, and the ground shall give its produce, 
and the heavens shall give their dew. 
And I will cause the remnant of this people to possess all these things.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
13:
And as 
you have been a byword of cursing among the nations, O house of Judah and house of Israel, 
so will I save you, and 
you shall be a blessing. 
Fear not, but 
let your hands be strong.”
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
14:
For thus says the 
Lord
 of hosts: 
“As I purposed to bring disaster to you when your fathers provoked me to wrath, and I did not relent, says the 
Lord
 of hosts,
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion

so again have I purposed in these days to bring good to Jerusalem and to the house of Judah; 
fear not.
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
16: The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion
Cited Eph. 4:25; See Ps. 15:2; See ch. 7:9; ver. 19

do not devise evil in your hearts against one another, and 
love no false oath, for all these things I hate, declares the 
Lord
.”
The Coming Peace and Prosperity of Zion

Lord
 of hosts came to me, saying,

Lord
 of hosts: The fast of the

seasons of joy and gladness and cheerful feasts. Therefore love

Lord
 of hosts: Peoples shall yet come, even the inhabitants of many cities.

 and to seek the

 of hosts in Jerusalem and

.

 of hosts: In those days
